A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a pre-revenue European regulated-intermediary SaaS startup building DORA or IDD compliance tooling for insurance distribution at pre-seed stage.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European fintech startup integrating with Open Banking or PSD2 APIs to automate payment reconciliation or AML screening for regulated payment providers.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Minalea (anchor)
2. Colosseum (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

MapRecruit.ai30NO MATCHThe company, MapRecruit.ai, provides an AI-powered recruitment platform. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 3 companies → evaluated: 2 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run
THIS RUN — 1 entry for Profund VC:
Week 20 → angle: ai_workflow_automationParis | Artificial Intelligence | 1-10 employees
ⓘ operatingStatus=Active · companyType=For Profit · Country auto-filled from city.

ai_workflow_automation weight=3 ACTIVE NOW — 7 entries

in 1d — Tue May 12Tuesday:Paris | Machine Learning | 11-50 empl.

in 8d — Tue May 19Tuesday:Paris | Compliance | 11-50 empl.
in 9d — Wed May 20Wednesday:Paris | Insurance | 1-10 empl.

Apify raw: 0 → 0 companies found
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Cnimsm0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. CNM Ingenuity offers resources, mentorship, funding, and programs for entrepreneurs and small business owners in New Mexico, which is an accelerator/incubator model, not a B2B software product.https://www.cnmingenuity.org/program/small-businessifted.eu/feed
Siobv Xnsocisrwr10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company provides soccer/football scores, which is a B2C sports content service, not a B2B software product for professional or institutional buyers.https://ng.soccerway.com/sifted.eu/feed
Cuodxc0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — The provided DESCRIPTION states DXC collaborates with STARTUP AUTOBAHN, selects emerging technology companies, and launches pilots. It does not describe what Cuodxc (the company in question) itself does, or if it has a product.https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/dxc-advansifted.eu/feed
HalQa10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. HalQa is a B2C platform for Quran learning, not a B2B software product aligned with workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. The business model is entirely different.https://www.halqa.online/sifted.eu/feed
Defense AI10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed (vertical/sector gate): The company is a defense company providing AI-enabled systems, which does not align with B2B SaaS for workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. The focus is on defense and autonomous systems rather than enterprise software for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, or regulated intermediaries.https://helsing.ai/sifted.eu/feed
James Frew10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. James Frew is a provider of building services such as plumbing, heating, and electrical services, which is not an early-stage B2B software product.https://www.jamesfrew.co.uk/about-us/sifted.eu/feed
Kelluu20NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 210 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

Moneybox10NO MATCHMoneybox is a B2C retail investment app, failing STEP A which requires a B2B software product.https://www.moneyboxapp.com/about-us
Cleo10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://web.meetcleo.com/page/term-conditions
Legora25NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company is in the legal tech sector providing AI tools for legal work, which aligns with AI workflow automation. However, the available information does not indicate that the product is sold to French SMEs or mid-market companies, nor does it specify a focus on French regulated intermediaries. Therefore, it does not meet the geographic or specific vertical criteria for the angles.https://legora.com/newsroom
Hugging Face30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Hugging Face provides tools and platforms for building and deploying AI models, not a B2B software product primarily sold to professional or institutional buyers for workflow automation or regulated intermediary purposes. It's a platform for AI developers, not a B2B SaaS for specific enterprise workflows.https://huggingface.co/blog
SAP0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails. SAP is a well-established, multinational enterprise software vendor, not an early-stage startup.https://www.sap.com/suisse/about/startups.html
Prior Labs20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides a frontier model for structured data (TabPFN) and deployment options, primarily focusing on AI infrastructure and models, not explicitly B2B SaaS for workflow automation or regulated intermediaries as required by the thesis. The mention of industries like finance and healthcare is generic.https://priorlabs.ai/blog-posts/priorlabs-next-cha
Supercell10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE B2B software product) failed. Supercell is a mobile games company, which is B2C entertainment, not a B2B software product.https://www.licenseglobal.com/video-games/supercel
Remy AI10NO MATCHThe company builds AI-powered robots, which falls under hardware/robotics and is explicitly listed as a REJECT CRITERIA for the AI workflow automation angle.https://f4.fund/startups/tryremyv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

sforbt
5 MATCH · 60 NO MATCH · 8% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
2. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 9d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 10d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 11d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 12d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 13d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 14d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 15d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 16d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 17d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 18d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 19d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 20d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 21d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 22d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 23d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 24d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 25d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 26d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 27d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
★ TODAY“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 1d — Tue May 12“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 2d — Wed May 13“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 3d — Thu May 14“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 4d — Fri May 15“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 5d — Sat May 16“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 6d — Sun May 17“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 7d — Mon May 18“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 8d — Tue May 19“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Digital Travel (anchor)
2. Umberto AI (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 3 companies → evaluated: 2 for sforbt
ALL 32 QUERIES — sforbt (all active every day):

LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.

Cnimsm provides resources and mentorship for startups and small businesses in New Mexico, which is a generic business incubator/accelerator model, not a European corporate travel technology company.https://www.cnmingenuity.org/program/small-businessifted.eu/feed

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 210 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
